source_jobs.title: Head of Accounting
source_jobs.description:
HEAD OF ACCOUNTING

San Francisco, CA




WHO WE ARE

At Vizcom, we’re reimagining how physical products are designed by fusing AI with human creativity to help designers move from sketch to reality at lightning speed. We’re looking for a Product Designer who obsesses over visual craft and interaction details, but also thrives on breaking new ground. This role will shape the future of our AI-powered sketching and collaboration tools, building experiences that feel as natural as drawing and as powerful as imagining something into existence. You’ll join a small, fast-moving team that values initiative, experimentation, and an eye for beauty.




THE ROLE

This is a rare build-from-scratch opportunity: own accounting at a fast-growing, AI-native company and shape it from the ground up. As our first dedicated accounting hire, you’ll design the systems, policies, and modern tech stack our financial data lives on. You’ll start as our Head of Accounting, hands-on and building, with a clear path to grow into a full VP, Accounting/Controller as the function and team scale beneath you.

Within the first few months, you’ll own the numbers and transform our enterprise billing and revenue recognition processes into an automated, best-in-class system. Within your first year, you’ll bring the close in-house, choose and implement a modern ERP, and lead us through our first external audit. Good foundations exist today, but from here you own accounting and build it. This isn’t a role where you manage a big team on day one. It’s one where you roll up your sleeves and build alongside a company of builders and designers, then grow the team as the work demands. You’ll report to the VP, Finance & Operations, and are preferably based in the San Francisco Bay Area, working in our hybrid environment with regular time together in our S.F. office.

If you’ve seen companies scale through $100M+ in revenue, if you’re in the #2 seat today and ready to step it up, if you’re ready to own the function and build it with AI in mind, this is the role for you.




HOW YOU’LL DO IT

 - Own enterprise billing and revenue recognition end-to-end. Turn quote-to-cash and ASC 606 revenue recognition into a clean, best-in-class motion that leadership and the board can trust.

 - Own and accelerate the close. Streamline monthly and quarterly close with our outsourced firm, then bring core functions (GL, reconciliations, AR/AP, close) in-house on a schedule you set, toward a predictable sub-10-day (or better) cadence.

 - Choose and implement our ERP. Within your first 6-9 months, evaluate legacy options (NetSuite) against AI-native platforms (Campfire, Rillet) against our real requirements, make the call, and then own the implementation, building the function AI-native from the start.

 - Get us audit-ready, then lead the audit. Stand up GAAP policies and a right-sized controls framework, clean historical financials, and select and manage the auditor through Vizcom’s first external audit.

 - Build the team and the org over time. As volume grows, define and hire the first accounting roles (GL Accountants, Revenue Accountants, and beyond) and develop the team (including AI Agents) you’ll eventually lead.

 - Partner across the business. With the VP, Finance & Operations on reporting, board, and investor support. With GTM, Product, and Data on the systems that connect accounting to the company. And with external tax, compliance, and outsourced partners.
   
   


WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 8-12 years of progressive accounting experience, with meaningful time at a venture-backed SaaS/AI company.

 - You’ve built or substantially leveled up an accounting function before. You’ve gone from outsourced or messy to structured and know what the journey actually looks like through $100M+ in revenue

 - Deep US GAAP expertise, with real ASC 606 revenue recognition experience for SaaS and enterprise contracts. You can write the policy and defend it to an auditor.

 - Hands-on ownership of t

source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
Sales Development Representative

Location: San Francisco (Hybrid, in-office 3 days/week)



Who We Are:

At Vizcom, we strive to be a powerful ally to designers, augmenting their skills and streamlining their workflow. Our innovative platform serves as a bridge between imagination and realization, enabling designers to bring their visions to life with unprecedented ease and precision. By combining the intuition of human creativity with the capabilities of artificial intelligence, Vizcom is shaping the future of design – one where technology amplifies rather than diminishes the designer's role.



The Role:
As a Sales Development Representative, you’ll be tasked with lead generation, prospecting, qualification, and appointment setting to help drive the sales pipeline, a critical part of our business. In this role, we’d expect you to be based in San Francisco, CA, helping to support our global sales team and generate new meetings.



How You’ll Do It:

 - Lead Generation: Identify new sales opportunities through outbound email, social media, and calling.

 - Inbound Lead Distribution: Help the sales team reach out to inbound leads and set meetings.

 - Qualification: Qualify leads based on criteria such as budget, authority, need, and timeline (BANT).

 - Appointment Setting: Schedule meetings or demos for Account Executives.

 - CRM Management: Track and manage all activities, leads, and opportunities in our CRM system.

 - Collaboration: Work closely with marketing and sales teams to establish and test lead generation strategies.



What You’ll Bring:

 - Experience: 1-2 years in a similar role, preferably in the tech or software industry.

 - Skills:
   
   - Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
   
   - Experience with CRM tools (e.g., Salesforce).
   
   - Understanding of outbound sales strategies and tactics.
   
   - Ability to handle rejection and stay motivated.

 - Qualities:
   
   - Highly motivated and goal-oriented.
   
   - Quick learner and adaptable to changes.
   
   - Team player with a proactive attitude.

 - Culture Fit:
   
   - You enjoy planning, executing, winning, and celebrating as a team.
   
   - Experience working remotely and/or in a startup environment.
   
   - Willingness to spend 2-3 days per week at our office in San Francisco.



Benefits While Working At Vizcom:

 - 100% employer-sponsored medical care for employees, 25% towards dependents.

 - Dental, Vision, and Mental Health Care.

 - Equity for all full-time, U.S.-based employees.

 - Flexible PTO.

 - Annual Learning & Development allowance

 - 401(k) with payroll match.

 - Paid Parental Leave.

 



Benefits While Working At Vizcom:

 - 100% employer sponsored medical care for employees, 25% towards dependents

 - Dental, VIsion and Mental Health Care

 - Equity

 - Generous Learning & Development Allowance

 - Flexible PTO

 - 401(k) with payroll match

 

Compensation:

The targeted pay for this position is $110,304 OTE.

 

The actual offer, including the compensation package, is determined based on multiple factors, including experience, location, and other business considerations. The overall package described in this post applies to W2, U.S. based employees- final package will be determined by local requirements and employment laws and accessibility.



*Please note, as part of our SOC2 Type 2 compliance, all employees are required to submit to a background check



Join Us and Make an Impact: At Vizcom, we move fast, offer meaningful equity ownership, and provide a compelling growth trajectory for our team members. We believe in the art of industrial design and strive to improve our world through accelerated visionary processes. Join us in shaping a world designed by you.

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer – Backend
source_jobs.description:
Agency Notice: We are not currently working with recruiting agencies for this role. Please do not contact Vizcom employees regarding this position. Any resumes submitted without a prior agreement will be considered unsolicited.




WHY VIZCOM

Our mission is to reshape design by streamlining the path from concept to creation, bringing more impactful ideas into the physical world.

We’re well-funded (Series B $51m to date), growing double-digits MoM, and expanding the core engineering team in SF. The surface area is big: realtime collaboration, GPU inference at scale, a modern TypeScript stack, and serving real enterprise




THE ROLE

As the Senior Software Engineer – Backend (Systems / Infrastructure) you’ll architect and deliver backend systems that keep Vizcom fast, reliable, and scalable as our workload and customer base grow. You’ll work on everything from queueing GPU workloads to optimizing GraphQL resolvers and streaming realtime events. You’ll have deep ownership over APIs, performance, and data integrity — while collaborating with frontend and AI teams to enable magical experiences. Within your first 90 days you will: ship one critical backend feature (e.g., model queue, caching layer, or realtime service), reduce p95 latency in one major API path by ~30%, Author one technical RFC proposing an improvement to architecture or reliability, Implement or document one observability improvement (metrics, traces, alerts).




COMPENSATION
$195,000 - $225,000 ANNUALLY + OFFERS EQUITY




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Design and implement scalable APIs in TypeScript/Node with GraphQL and Postgres

 - Profile and optimize slow paths — queries, resolvers, and I/O

 - Build and operate distributed systems: job queues, streaming, caching, and observability

 - Collaborate with AI engineers to integrate GPU inference pipelines into user workflows

 - Improve reliability: lead incident reviews, strengthen CI/CD and deployment flows

 - Mentor teammates on backend architecture, testing, and performance thinking




THE PROBLEMS YOU’LL TACKLE

 - Distributed GPU inference and queueing with bursty workloads.

 - Realtime synchronization with WebSocket and CRDT-based collaboration.

 - GraphQL performance: caching, batching, resolver optimization.

 - Secure multi-tenant data boundaries and enterprise integrations (SSO/SAML/SCIM).




OUR STACK (TODAY)

 - Frontend: TypeScript, React, Vite, WebGL; realtime collaboration.

 - API/Backend: TypeScript/Node, GraphQL (PostGraphile), Postgres, Redis, background workers.

 - Infra: Kubernetes, Pulumi, CI/CD with GitHub Actions, Datadog for observability, feature flags.

 - Security/Enterprise: SSO/SAML (WorkOS), SOC 2-minded practices.

*Experience with every item above is not required. What matters is that you are excited to shape it.





QUALIFICATIONS

 - 5–8+ years building production web systems (TypeScript/Node or similar)

 - Strength in databases, performance tuning, and distributed systems

 - Experience operating Kubernetes or similar orchestration environments.

 - Fluency with observability tools and debugging live systems.

 - Bonus: GPU workloads, background job systems, or realtime architectures.
   
   


HOW WE WORK

 - Small team, high ownership. Fewer people, more leverage.

 - Design-centric. We obsess over speed and feel; “fast enough” usually is not.

 - Write it down. RFCs, crisp decisions, living docs.

 - Operate what you build. Everyone takes on-call support; incidents drive improvement, not blame.



INTERVIEW PROCESS

 - Intro (30m): mutual fit, role and impact.

 - Architectural deep-dive (45m): product and design collaboration.

 - Coding Session (45m):

 - Onsite Interview (half day)

 - References and offer.


READY TO BUILD?

If you want your work to be used daily by world-class designers and you like owning problems end to end, let’s talk. Apply with links (GitHub, LinkedIn, notable projects) and a short note on something hard you shipped.

source_jobs.title: Senior Platform & Reliability Engineer (SRE)
source_jobs.description:
Agency Notice: We are not currently working with recruiting agencies for this role. Please do not contact Vizcom employees regarding this position. Any resumes submitted without a prior agreement will be considered unsolicited.



About Vizcom

Vizcom is a visual creation platform that combines modern web tooling with AI-powered workflows. Our stack includes React/TypeScript frontend, Node/Koa + PostGraphile API services, PostgreSQL, Redis, BullMQ queues, and Kubernetes-based production infrastructure.

We’re hiring a senior owner of stability and infrastructure to ensure the platform is reliable, fast, and resilient as we scale.

Role Mission

Own service reliability end-to-end: prevent incidents, reduce blast radius when failures happen, and lead fast, high-quality recovery when production degrades.

This is a hands-on technical leadership role with authority to set reliability standards and enforce production guardrails.

Compensation

$200,000 – $250,000 base salary + meaningful equity


What You’ll Own

 - Reliability bar: Set and enforce SLIs/SLOs/error budgets for critical user flows.

 - Production architecture resilience: Drive failure isolation across API, workers, queues, and dependencies so one subsystem cannot take down core access.

 - Kubernetes runtime reliability: Define probe contracts, rollout/rollback standards, graceful shutdown behavior, scaling/resource policies, and startup safety.

 - Queue + job safety (BullMQ/Redis): Own poison pill containment and workload isolation.

 - Incident command quality: Lead Sev1/Sev2 response end-to-end (containment, communications, technical direction, RCA, corrective action execution).

 - Reliability operating system: Own observability quality (signals over noise), on-call effectiveness, runbooks, and postmortem discipline.

 - Release safety authority: Gate risky deploys and enforce reliability guardrails when production health is at risk.
   
   

Traits We’re Looking For

 - Calm, structured incident commander under pressure.

 - Thinks in failure modes and blast radius by default.

 - Pragmatic: can stabilize quickly, then implement durable fixes.

 - High ownership and strong written communication.
   


FIRST 90 DAYS

 - Establish baseline reliability metrics and identify top platform risks.

 - Tighten incident response mechanics (roles, comms cadence, runbooks, status updates).

 - Deliver high-impact hardening fixes across probes/startup paths/queue safety.

 - Publish a prioritized 6–12 month reliability roadmap with clear ownership and milestones.



If possible please include one incident you personally led and send to Jordan@vizcom.com :

1) what failed,

2) how you contained it,

3) what permanent fixes you shipped, and measured.

source_jobs.title: Senior Analytics Engineer
source_jobs.description:
SENIOR ANALYTICS ENGINEER

Location: San Francisco, CA (Hybrid Role)



Who We Are:

At Vizcom, we're reimagining how physical products are designed by fusing AI with human creativity to help designers move from sketch to reality at lightning speed. We're a small, fast-moving team that values initiative, experimentation, and an eye for beauty. As a learning-driven company, we believe good decisions come from good data, and we're building a foundation where both humans and AI can turn that data into smarter ones.



The Role:

As our Senior Analytics Engineer, you'll be the second hire on the data team, working alongside the Head of Data as the primary data partner to Product, Finance, Marketing, and Sales. You'll work across the stack: designing pipelines and semantic models in BigQuery and Omni, then using that foundation to build the dashboards and run the analyses that shape

how we price, prioritize, and grow. This is a role for someone who genuinely enjoys moving between analyst and engineer work, and who wants to help build a data function from the ground up rather than inherit one.

 

You'll also play a central role in how Vizcom uses AI internally. Our BI stack is built around Claude and Omni, and we need someone who can shape the semantic layer so both humans and AI agents can get reliable, trustworthy answers from it.




How You’ll Do It:



 - Partner with Product, Finance, Marketing, and Sales to scope what matters, define metrics, and translate business questions into analyses.

 - Partner with engineering on product instrumentation. Help decide what events we capture and how they're structured to enable robust analysis.

 - Own the analysis end-to-end, from data modeling through dashboards, readouts, and ongoing monitoring.

 - Build and maintain the semantic layer in Omni so both humans and AI agents (Claude) can query the business reliably. Govern metric definitions, resolve discrepancies between sources, and make self-serve the default path.

 - Design and maintain data pipelines in BigQuery to support analytics and reporting, including ETL for new data sources as they come online.

 - Inform architectural decisions on transformation, orchestration, and tooling as the stack matures.

 - Partner with the Head of Data on prioritization, help shape the team's roadmap, and identify opportunities to improve how we work.





What You’ll Bring:



 - 5 - 7 years of experience in analytics, analytics engineering, or a hybrid role, with specific examples of dashboards and analyses that drove business decisions.

 - Fluent SQL and hands-on experience with BigQuery or a comparable cloud warehouse.

 - Technically fluent beyond SQL. You're comfortable in a codebase, reason about how systems fit together, and contribute substantively to architectural conversations with engineering. Hands-on experience in a general-purpose language like Python or TypeScript.

 - Semantic modeling experience in Omni, LookML, Cube, MetricFlow, or similar. Omni experience is a strong plus.

 - Solid grounding in database architecture and ETL, with hands-on experience designing pipelines.

 - Experience analyzing product feature launches: defining success metrics, reviewing instrumentation, and calling the results as they are.

 - Strong stakeholder skills. You can gather requirements from non-technical partners, push back thoughtfully when the ask and the real need diverge, and communicate findings clearly in both writing and conversation.

 - You've integrated AI meaningfully into how you work. You can speak specifically to how you use it, where it works, where it doesn't, and how you've developed judgment about when to trust output.

 - Comfortable switching between analyst and engineer modes, and across domains. Product retention one day, finance or marketing performance the next.

 - As a colleague, you enjoy planning, executing, winning and celebrating as a team

 - Experience working remotely and/or in a startup environment



Benefit

source_jobs.title: Executive Assistant To The CEO
source_jobs.description:
Executive Assistant to the CEO

Location: San Francisco, CA (in-office)



Who We Are: At Vizcom, we're reimagining how physical products are designed by fusing AI with human creativity to help designers move from sketch to reality at lightning speed. We're a small, fast-moving team that values initiative, experimentation, and an eye for beauty - and we're scaling quickly. As we grow, our CEO needs a trusted right hand to help him operate at the highest level. That's where you come in.



The Role: As the Executive Assistant to the CEO, you'll be the operational backbone of our CEO's day-to-day life, owning his calendar, travel, communications, and the countless behind-the-scenes details that keep him focused on what matters most: building Vizcom. This is a high-trust, high-context role that blends classic EA work (calendar, travel, expenses) with strategic coordination (board meetings, investor relationships) and light personal support. You'll be one of the first people he talks to in the morning and one of the last at night, and you'll have a front-row seat to how an AI startup gets built. The ideal candidate is unflappable, anticipatory, discreet, and obsessed with making the people around them more effective.



How You'll Do It:



 - Own and prioritize the CEO's calendar - balancing internal meetings, customer and investor commitments, deep work blocks, and personal time

 - Coordinate complex domestic and international travel, including flights, accommodations, ground transportation, and detailed itineraries

 - Manage board meeting logistics end-to-end: scheduling, materials prep coordination, dinners, travel for board members, and follow-up communications

 - Serve as a thoughtful liaison between the CEO and investors, board members, customers, and the broader Vizcom team

 - Draft, proofread, and send communications on the CEO's behalf with a tone that matches his voice

 - Process expenses, reimbursements, and credit card reconciliations in a timely and organized way

 - Handle personal and lifestyle support including errands, appointments, household coordination, and family scheduling as needed

 - Anticipate needs before they're voiced

 - Maintain absolute discretion with confidential business, financial, and personal information

 - Partner with broader operations leadership on cross-functional projects and CEO-led initiatives as they arise (you’ll be working closely with our Head of People & Culture as well as our VP of Finance & Operations)



What You'll Bring:



 - 4-6 years of experience as an Executive Assistant, ideally supporting a CEO, founder, or other C-suite executive

 - Experience in a high-growth startup environment, where priorities shift quickly and there's no playbook

 - Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, with strong judgment about when to write vs. call vs. text

 - Mastery of Google Workspace, Slack, and modern productivity tools - bonus points for an interest in AI-powered workflows

 - Comfort with ambiguity and a bias toward action; you'd rather ship something 90% right than wait for 100%

 - Discretion and emotional intelligence - you know what to share, what to hold, and how to read a room

 - Willingness to be on-call for time-sensitive issues outside of standard business hours when needed

 - Based in the San Francisco Bay Area and able to work in-office alongside the CEO

 - As a colleague, you enjoy planning, executing, winning and celebrating as a team




Benefits While Working At Vizcom:



 - 100% employer sponsored medical care for employees, 25% towards dependents

 - Dental, Vision and Mental Health Care

 - Equity

 - Flexible PTO

 - 401(k) with payroll match

 - Annual Learning & Development allowance


The salary for this role is between $125K - $145K, depending on experience. This role is eligible for equity.

source_jobs.title: Product Support Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Product Support Engineer



Who We Are:

At Vizcom, we’re reimagining how physical products are designed by fusing AI with human creativity to help designers move from sketch to reality at lightning speed. We’re looking for a Product Designer who obsesses over visual craft and interaction details, but also thrives on breaking new ground. This role will shape the future of our AI-powered sketching and collaboration tools, building experiences that feel as natural as drawing and as powerful as imagining something into existence. You’ll join a small, fast-moving team that values initiative, experimentation, and an eye for beauty.



The Role:

In the role of Product Support Engineer, you will bridge the gap between our customers, our customer success team, and our engineering organization. You will work directly with users to diagnose, troubleshoot, and resolve complex technical issues while developing a deep structural understanding of Vizcom’s product and infrastructure.

This isn't a passive ticketing role. You will combine sharp software engineering skills with empathetic communication to investigate root causes and, where possible, implement solutions directly in the codebase. For deeper architectural changes, you will partner closely with core Engineering to ensure swift resolution.


How You’ll Do It:



 - Investigate & Resolve: Troubleshoot, reproduce, and resolve complex product and workflow issues escalated via chat, email, and community channels.

 - Ship Code: Write code, submit pull requests (PRs), and make direct changes to the codebase to fix customer bugs, improve error handling, and prevent recurring pain points in coordination with the Engineering team.

 - Build Internal Tooling: Design and build lightweight scripts, internal dashboards, and automations to optimize Customer Success operations and empower the team to scale.

 - Bridge the Gap: Translate vague customer complaints into highly detailed, actionable engineering tickets with clear reproduction steps, logs, and technical context.

 - On-Call & Incident Management: Be a thoughtful partner during critical, high-severity production incidents, collaborating with DevOps and Core Engineering on communications, monitoring, debugging and assessing customer impact

 - Define Metrics & SLAs: Track, analyze, and report on support engineering metrics (e.g., time-to-resolution, escalation rates) to continually improve team efficiency and product health.

 - Advocate for the User: Analyze recurring technical issues to identify systemic product flaws and share data-driven insights with Product and Engineering to influence our roadmap.

 - Manage Infrastructure & Security: Assist enterprise clients with setting up and debugging SSO, authentication, networking configurations, CORS issues, and integration workflows.

 - Onboarding & Mentorship: Help onboard new technical support team members and establish engineering-backed training programs to elevate the broader tier-1 support team's technical capabilities.

 - Knowledge Sharing: Author internal technical documentation, troubleshooting playbooks, and external knowledge base articles to scale self-service resolution.
   
   



What You’ll Bring:



 - Experience: 3–5+ years in Product Support Engineering, Technical Support, Solutions Engineering, or a highly technical, customer-facing software role.

 - Technical Chops: Strong coding skills in at least one modern programming language—ideally JavaScript/TypeScript or Python.

 - Modern Web Fluency: Deep experience debugging web applications, authentication flows (SAML/OIDC), networking protocols, and cloud-based software architectures.

 - Detective Skills: Proficiency in using browser developer tools, server logs (e.g., Datadog, LogRocket), SQL, and other monitoring tools to pinpoint root causes.

 - Tooling Ecosystem: Experience navigating modern support and engineering ecosystems (e.g., Intercom, Linear, GitHub).

 - Communication & Empathy: Exceptional written com

source_jobs.title: Senior Enterprise Account Executive - West
source_jobs.description:
Midmarket Account Executive

Location: San Francisco (Hybrid, in office 3 x week)

Who We Are:

At Vizcom, we're committed to creating products grounded in strong technical principles. Our approach is defined by a commitment to future-focused, high-quality coding, small yet highly efficient teams, practical tooling, and custom-tailored best practices. We emphasize future ease of maintenance over immediate coding convenience, trust and accountability within our team, and an understanding of our product and users. As a learning-driven team, we continually explore new technologies and approaches, ensuring our work is transparent and that each team member has a significant impact.



The Role:

As a Midmarket Account Executive you’ll grow Vizcom’s business in key geographic locations.

Location: Remote work


How You’ll Do It:



 - Identify and target potential clients within assigned territories

 - Develop and execute strategic sales plans to achieve revenue targets

 - Build and maintain strong relationships with key decision-makers and stakeholders

 - Conduct thorough market research to understand customer needs and industry trends

 - Present and demonstrate our products/services to prospective clients

 - Negotiate and close high-value deals, ensuring customer satisfaction and long-term partnerships

 - Collaborate with internal teams, including marketing, product development, and customer support, to ensure a seamless customer experience

 - Keep accurate records of sales activities and update CRM systems regularly

 - Attend industry events, conferences, and networking opportunities to generate leads and expand the customer base

 - Traveling to customer onsite meetings, monthly/quarterly

 - Handling new logo, expansion, and renewal opportunities







What You’ll Bring:



 - Minimum of 2 years of experience in mid-market sales.

 - Proven track record of meeting or exceeding sales targets

 - Strong understanding of sales processes and techniques

 - Excellent communication, negotiation, and presentation skills

 - Ability to build and maintain relationships with C-level executives and decision-makers

 - Self-motivated, results-oriented, and able to work independently

 - Willingness to travel

 - As a colleague, you enjoy planning, executing, winning and celebrating as a team

 - Experience working remotely and/or in a startup environment



Benefits While Working At Vizcom:

 - 100% employer sponsored medical for employees, 25% towards dependents

 - Dental, Vision and Mental Health Care

 - Equity for all full-time, U.S.-based employees

 - Flexible PTO

 - 401(k) with match

 - Paid Parental Leave

 - Annual Learning & Development Allowance

 

Compensation:

The target OTE for this position is: $200K - $250K OTE (inclusive of base + commissions). This role is eligible for equity. The actual offer, including the compensation package, is determined based on multiple factors, including experience, location, and other business considerations.



*Please note that as part of our SOC2 Type 2 compliance, all employees must submit to a background check.

Enterprise Account Executive

Location: San Francisco (Hybrid, in office 3 x week)



Who We Are:

At Vizcom, we're committed to creating products grounded in strong technical principles. Our approach is defined by a commitment to future-focused, high-quality coding, small yet highly efficient teams, practical tooling, and custom-tailored best practices. We emphasize future ease of maintenance over immediate coding convenience, trust and accountability within our team, and an understanding of our product and users. source_jobs.title: Senior Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Agency Notice: We are not currently working with recruiting agencies for this role. Please do not contact Vizcom employees regarding this position. Any resumes submitted without a prior agreement will be considered unsolicited.




WHY VIZCOM

Our mission is to reshape design by streamlining the path from concept to creation, bringing more impactful ideas into the physical world.

We’re well-funded (Series B $51m to date), growing double-digits MoM, and expanding the core engineering team in SF. The surface area is big: realtime collaboration, GPU inference at scale, a modern TypeScript stack, and serving real enterprise




THE ROLE

As the Senior Product Engineer you’ll own major product surfaces in Vizcom’s real-time design environment — from our canvas rendering engine to collaborative UX systems. You’ll work across React, WebGL, and GraphQL, shaping performance, interaction design, and developer experience. You’ll partner closely with designers, AI engineers, and backend leads to make Vizcom’s interface both beautiful and blazingly fast. Within your first 90 days you will: ship one new or re-architected user-facing feature that improves product performance or fidelity, establish a render or collaboration performance baseline (e.g., frame rate, latency, bundle size), propose or contribute to frontend tech direction (e.g., render pipeline abstraction, UI state model), and reduce one persistent performance or UX friction point across the frontend stack.




COMPENSATION
$195,000 - $225,000 ANNUALLY + OFFERS EQUITY




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Build rich interactive surfaces: own features that blend design, collaboration, and 3D visualization

 - Optimize performance: push React and WebGL rendering to feel instant under load

 - Craft developer ergonomics: shape component architecture, hooks, and state models that scale

 - Collaborate early with Design: prototype fast, test feel, and bring high-fidelity UX to life

 - Improve observability: instrument and debug perf metrics (FPS, React profiler, network traces)

 - Mentor teammates: review code for clarity, structure, and maintainability
   
   


THE PROBLEMS YOU’LL TACKLE

 - High-fanout realtime collaboration with CRDT-style data models

 - Rendering pipelines in WebGL/WebGPU, balancing fidelity and speed

 - Bundle optimization, lazy loading, and Vite build performance

 - Complex interaction logic that must still feel intuitive and instant




OUR STACK (TODAY)

 - Frontend: TypeScript, React, Vite, WebGL; realtime collaboration.

 - API/Backend: TypeScript/Node, GraphQL (PostGraphile), Postgres, Redis, background workers.

 - Infra: Kubernetes, Pulumi, CI/CD with GitHub Actions, Datadog for observability, feature flags.

 - Security/Enterprise: SSO/SAML (WorkOS), SOC 2-minded practices.

*Experience with every item above is not required. What matters is that you are excited to shape it.





QUALIFICATIONS

 - 5–8+ years building and shipping complex frontend systems (React/TypeScript)

 - Strong experience with WebGL, Canvas, or custom rendering engines

 - Deep understanding of UI performance, profiling, and memory management

 - Strong eye for design quality — you care about both pixels and milliseconds

 - As a colleague, you enjoy planning, executing, winning and celebrating as a team

 - Experience working remotely and/or in a startup environment

 - Bonus: experience with GraphQL, WASM, GPU pipelines, or 3D scene systems

How We Work

 - Small team, high ownership. Fewer people, more leverage.

 - Design-centric. We obsess over speed and feel; “fast enough” usually is not.

 - Write it down. RFCs, crisp decisions, living docs.

 - Operate what you build. Everyone takes on-call support; incidents drive improvement, not blame.



INTERVIEW PROCESS

 - Intro (30m): mutual fit, role and impact.

 - Architectural deep-dive (45m): product and design collaboration.

 - Coding Session (45m):

 - Onsite Interview (half day)

 - References and offer.
